reasoning: If 4 o'clock takes 6 seconds to strike, then each strike takes 6/4 = 1.5 seconds.
To strike 12 o'clock, it would take 12 * 1.5 = 18 seconds.
When a clock strikes 4 o'clock, there are 4 strikes but only 3 intervals between the strikes (4 - 1 = 3 intervals).
The clock takes 6 seconds to complete these 3 intervals, meaning each interval takes 6 seconds / 3 intervals = 2 seconds.
When the clock strikes 12 o'clock, there are 12 strikes, which means there are 11 intervals (12 - 1 = 11 intervals).
Therefore, the total time taken to strike 12 o'clock will be 11 intervals * 2 seconds/interval = 22 seconds.
